blocks:
Surah 'Abasa

Makkan, and it is 42 verses

Surah 'Abasa

And it is called Surah al-Safarah (سورة السّفرة). Makkan. Its number of verses is forty-two according to the Hijazi and Kufi counts, forty-one according to the Basri count, and forty according to the Shami and the First Madani counts. Its points of difference (اختلافها) are three verses: وَلِأَنْعامِكُمْ إِلى طَعامِهِ ** and *الصَّاخَّةُ بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ* (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) عَبَسَ وَتَوَلَّى. (1) He frowned and turned away (٢) أَنْ جاءَهُ الْأَعْمى (2) because the blind man came to him.

Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning Uthman and Ibn Umm Maktum. Ibn Umm Maktum was the mu'adhdhin (مؤذّناً) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and he was blind. He came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم while his Companions were with Him, and Uthman was present. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم gave him precedence over Uthman, so Uthman frowned with his face and turned away from him. Thus, Allah revealed: ** عَبَسَ وَتَوَلَّى. (He frowned and turned away), meaning Uthman, ** أَنْ جاءَهُ الْأَعْمى (because the blind man came to him.)

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning a man from Banu Umayyah who was with the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. Then Ibn Umm Maktum came, and when the man saw him, he felt disgust (تقذر) towards him, drew himself in, frowned, and turned his face away from him. So, Allah, the Glorious, recounted that and condemned it upon him.

(٣) وَما يُدْرِيكَ لَعَلَّهُ يَزَّكَّى ** (3) What makes you know, maybe he would purify himself, al-Qummi said: that he would be pure (طاهراً) or purer (أزكى). (٤) أَوْ يَذَّكَّرُ ** (4) or be reminded, He said: the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم reminds him, فَتَنْفَعَهُ الذِّكْرى ** (so that the reminder may benefit him). And it was recited with the nasb (بالنصب) (accusative case). (٥) أَمَّا مَنِ اسْتَغْنى. (5) But as for him who is self-sufficient, (٦) فَأَنْتَ لَهُ تَصَدَّى ** (6) you attend to him. You expose yourself by turning towards him (تتعرّض بالإقبال عليه). al-Qummi then addressed Uthman and said: أَمَّا مَنِ اسْتَغْنى ** (But as for him who is self-sufficient) the Ayah. He said: When a rich man comes to you, you attend to him (تتصدّى له) and elevate him (وترفعه). (٧) وَما عَلَيْكَ أَلَّا يَزَّكَّى ** (7) and you are not to blame if he does not purify himself. He said: Meaning, you do not care whether he is pure (زكياً) or not pure (غير زكي) if he is rich (غنياً). (٨) وَأَمَّا مَنْ جاءَكَ يَسْعى ** (8) But as for him who comes to you eagerly, He said: Meaning Ibn Umm Maktum. (٩) وَهُوَ يَخْشى. (9) while he is fearful [of Allah], (١٠) فَأَنْتَ عَنْهُ تَلَهَّى ** (10) you ignore him. Meaning, you are distracted (تلهو) and do not turn your attention to him (ولا تلتفت إليه). And تصدّى was recited with the doubling (بتشديد) of the ṣād. And in al-Majma': And the recitation of al-Baqir عليه السلام is تصدّى with the ḍamma (بضمّ) on the tā' and the fatḥa (وفتح) on the ṣād, and تلهّى also with the ḍamma (بضمّ) on the tā'.

I say: As for what is well-known concerning the revelation (تنزيل) of these Ayat regarding the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم instead of Uthman, the context (سياق) of such reproaches (المعاتبات) that are unfitting for his station (منصبه) rejects it. And likewise, what is mentioned after that until the end of the Surah, as is not hidden from one who is knowledgeable of the styles of speech (أساليب الكلام). And it seems that it is among the fabrications (مختلفات) of the people of hypocrisy (أهل النفاق), may Allah forsake them (خذلهم الله).

(١١) كَلَّا ** A deterrent against the one being reproached and a repetition of the like of it. ** إِنَّها تَذْكِرَةٌ ** Al-Qummi said: It is the Quran. (11) No indeed! It is an admonition. (١٢) فَمَنْ شاءَ ذَكَرَهُ. (12) So let anyone who wishes remember it. (١٣) فِي صُحُفٍ مُكَرَّمَةٍ. (13) In honored scrolls, (١٤) مَرْفُوعَةٍ ** He said: He said, [they are] with Allah. ** مُطَهَّرَةٍ ** Purified, exalted above the hands of the devils (الشياطين). (14) Exalted, purified, (١٥) بِأَيْدِي سَفَرَةٍ ** It was said: meaning scribes (كتبة) from the angels (الملائكة) and the prophets (الأنبياء). And Al-Qummi said: By the hands of the Imams (الأئمّة). (15) In the hands of scribes, (١٦) كِرامٍ بَرَرَةٍ. (16) Noble and pious. In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The preserver of the Quran who acts upon it is with the noble and pious scribes (السفرة الكرام البررة). (١٧) قُتِلَ الْإِنْسانُ ما أَكْفَرَهُ ** A supplication against him with the most heinous of supplications, and an expression of astonishment at his excessiveness in ingratitude (الكفران). (17) Perish man! How ungrateful he is! In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Meaning, man is cursed (لعن الإِنسان). (١٨) مِنْ أَيِّ شَيْءٍ خَلَقَهُ ** The interrogation is for the purpose of belittlement (التحقير). (18) Of what did He create him? (١٩) مِنْ نُطْفَةٍ خَلَقَهُ فَقَدَّرَهُ ** So He prepared him for what is suitable for him, in terms of limbs (الأعضاء) and forms (الأشكال), stage by stage (أطوار), until his creation was completed. (19) He created him from a drop of semen, then proportioned him. (٢٠) ثُمَّ السَّبِيلَ يَسَّرَهُ ** Al-Qummi said: He made the path of goodness (طريق الخير) easy for him. (20) Then He made the way easy for him. (٢١) ثُمَّ أَماتَهُ فَأَقْبَرَهُ. (21) Then He makes him die and buries him. (٢٢) ثُمَّ إِذا شاءَ أَنْشَرَهُ ** The counting of death (الإِماتة) and burial (الإقبار) among the blessings (النّعم) is because death is, generally speaking, a connection (وصلة) to eternal life (الحياة الأبديّة) and pure pleasures (اللّذّات الخالصة), and the command for burial (الامر بالقبر) is an honor (تكرمة) and a protection from predators (السباع). (22) Then, when He wishes, He will raise him up. (٢٣) كَلَّا ** A deterrent for man concerning what he is upon. ** لَمَّا يَقْضِ ما أَمَرَهُ ** He has not yet fulfilled, from the time of Adam until this moment, all that Allah commanded him entirely, since no one is free from some shortcoming (تقصير ما). (23) No indeed! He has not yet carried out what He commanded him. (٢٤) فَلْيَنْظُرِ الْإِنْسانُ إِلى طَعامِهِ ** Following the intrinsic blessings (النعم الذاتية) with the extrinsic blessings (النّعم الخارجية). (24) Let man consider his food: (٢٥) أَنَّا صَبَبْنَا الْماءَ صَبًّا ** And it was read inna (انّا) with fathah (بالفتح). (25) We poured down water in abundance, (٢٦) ثُمَّ شَقَقْنَا الْأَرْضَ شَقًّا ** Meaning, with vegetation (بالنبات). (26) then We split the earth with a split, (٢٧) فَأَنْبَتْنا فِيها حَبًّا. (27) and grew in it grain, (٢٨) وَعِنَباً وَقَضْباً ** Meaning fresh fodder (الرطبة). Al-Qummi said: al-qadb (القضب) is lucerne (القتّ). (28) and grapes and fresh fodder, (٢٩) وَزَيْتُوناً وَنَخْلاً. (29) and olive and date-palm, (٣٠) وَحَدائِقَ غُلْباً ** Great (عظاماً). The gardens (الحدائق) are described thus due to their density (تكاثفها) and the abundance of their trees (أشجارها). (30) and luxuriant gardens, (٣١) وَفاكِهَةً وَأَبًّا ** And pasture (مرعى). Al-Qummi said: al-abb (الأبّ) is grass (الحشيش) for the livestock (البهائم). (31) and fruits and pasture, (٣٢) مَتاعاً لَكُمْ وَلِأَنْعامِكُمْ. (32) as a provision for you and your livestock. In Irshad al-Mufid: It is narrated that Abu Bakr was asked about the Almighty's saying: ** وَفاكِهَةً وَأَبًّا ** (and fruits and pasture), and he did not know the meaning of al-abb (الأبّ) from the Quran. He said: "What sky will shade me, or what earth will carry me, or what shall I do if I speak concerning the Book of Allah about what I do not know? As for the fruit (الفاكهة), we know it, but as for al-abb (الأبّ), Allah knows best about it." This statement reached the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), who said: "Glory be to Allah (سبحان الله)! Did he not know that al-abb (الأبّ) is fodder (الكلاء) and pasture (المرعى)? And that His saying, glory be to Him: ** وَفاكِهَةً وَأَبًّا ** is Allah counting His favor upon His creation regarding what He feeds them and what He created for them and their livestock, by which their souls are sustained and their bodies are maintained." In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was said to him regarding the Almighty's saying: فَلْيَنْظُرِ الْإِنْسانُ إِلى طَعامِهِ (Let man consider his food): "What is his food (طعامه)?" He said: "His knowledge (علمه) which he takes, and from whom he takes it." I say: That is because food (الطعام) encompasses both the food of the body and the food of the soul (الروح) together, just as man (الإنسان) encompasses both the body and the soul together. Just as he is commanded to look at his physical nourishment (غذائه الجسمانيّ) to know that it descended from the heaven, from Allah, glory be to Him, by pouring down water in abundance (صبّ الماء صبّاً) until the end of the verses, so too is he commanded to look at his spiritual nourishment (غذائه الرّوحاني)—which is knowledge (العلم)—to know that it descended from the heaven, from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, by pouring down the rains of revelation (امطار الوحي) onto the land of prophethood (أرض النبوّة), the tree of the Message (شجرة الرّسالة), and the spring of wisdom (ينبوع الحكمة). From this, He brought forth the seeds of realities (حبوب الحقائق) and the fruits of gnosis (فواكه المعارف), so that the souls of those capable of cultivation (التربية) may be nourished by them. Therefore, his saying: "His knowledge which he takes, and from whom he takes it," means that he ought to take his knowledge from the People of the House of Prophethood (أهل بيت النبوّة), who are the landing places of revelation (مهابط الوحي) and the springs of wisdom (ينابيع الحكمة), who take their knowledge from Allah, glory be to Him, so that it may be suitable to become nourishment for his soul, unlike others who have no connection between themselves and Allah in terms of revelation (الوحي) or inspiration (الإلهام). For their knowledge is either the memorization of the sayings of men whose statements hold no proof (حجّة), or it is a tool for argumentation (آلة جدال) that has no place in the clear path (المحجّة). Neither of these comes from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, but rather from Satan (الشيطان), and thus is not suitable as nourishment for the soul (الرّوح) and faith (الايمان). Since the tafsir (exegesis) of the verse is apparent, he did not address it, but rather addressed its ta’wil (esoteric interpretation). However, the verification (التحقيق) is that both meanings are intended by the word with a single application (بإطلاق واحد).

(٣٣) فَإِذا جاءَتِ الصَّاخَّةُ ** (33) When the deafening cry comes— meaning, the Blast (النفخة). It is described by this term metaphorically because people will raise a clamor (يضجّون) due to it. (٣٤) يَوْمَ يَفِرُّ الْمَرْءُ مِنْ أَخِيهِ. (34) the day when a man will flee from his brother, (٣٥) وَأُمِّهِ وَأَبِيهِ (35) and his mother and his father, (٣٦) وَصاحِبَتِهِ وَبَنِيهِ ** (36) and his spouse and his sons. due to his preoccupation with his own affair and his knowledge that they will not benefit him, or out of caution against their demanding from him what he fell short of regarding their rights. The delay of the dearest then the next dearest is for emphasis, as if it were said: يَفِرُّ الْمَرْءُ مِنْ أَخِيهِ ** (a man will flee from his brother), rather, from أُمِّهِ وَأَبِيهِ ** (his mother and his father), rather, from صاحِبَتِهِ وَبَنِيهِ. (his spouse and his sons).

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him), he said: A man stood up asking the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) about this verse: Who are they? He said: Qabil will flee from Habil (peace be upon them both). And the one who will flee from his mother is Musa (peace be upon him). And the one who will flee from his father is Ibrahim (peace be upon him)—meaning the nurturing father (الأب المربّي), not the biological father (الوالد). And the one who will flee from his spouse is Lut. And the one who will flee from his son is Nuh (peace be upon him) and his son Kan'an.

And in al-Khisal, narrated from al-Husayn ibn Ali (peace be upon them both): The like of it, without the saying: "meaning the nurturing father (الأب المربّي), not the biological father (الوالد)." And its compiler (مصنّفه) said: Musa (peace be upon him) will only flee from his mother out of fear that he may have fallen short in what was obligatory upon him regarding her right. And Ibrahim (peace be upon him) will only flee from the nurturing father (الأب المربّي) who was a polytheist (المشرك), not from the biological father (الأب الوالد). And this is a historical account (تاريخ).

(٣٧) لِكُلِّ امْرِئٍ مِنْهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ شَأْنٌ يُغْنِيهِ ** (On that day) every man among them will have a preoccupation that will suffice him. Al-Qummi said: [It means] a preoccupation (شغل) that preoccupies him from others. And in al-Majma', narrated from Sawdah, the wife of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: People will be resurrected barefoot, naked, and uncircumcised (عزلاً). Sweat will bridle them (يلجمهم) and reach the lobe of the ears. She said: I said, "O Messenger of Allah, what a disgrace (وا سوأتاه)! Will some of us look at others when that comes?" He said: "People will be preoccupied from that," and He recited this verse.

(٣٨) وُجُوهٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ مُسْفِرَةٌ ** (38) Faces on that day will be bright, luminous. (٣٩) ضاحِكَةٌ مُسْتَبْشِرَةٌ ** (39) laughing, joyous, due to what they see of the bliss (النّعيم). (٤٠) وَوُجُوهٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ عَلَيْها غَبَرَةٌ ** (40) and faces on that day will be covered with dust, dust and gloom (كدورة). (٤١) تَرْهَقُها قَتَرَةٌ ** (41) overcast by darkness. darkness and gloom (ظلمة) covers them. (٤٢) أُولئِكَ هُمُ الْكَفَرَةُ الْفَجَرَةُ ** (42) It is they who are the faithless, the vicious. those who combined wickedness (الفجور) with disbelief (الكفر); therefore, dust (الغبرة) is combined with the blackness (سواد) of their faces.

في ثواب الأعمال والمجمع عن الصادق عليه السلام : من قرأ عبس وتولّى وإذا الشمس كوّرت كانت تحت جناح الله من الجنان وفي ظلّ الله وكرامته وفي جنّاته ولا يعظم ذلك على الله إن شاء الله تعالى.

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites 'Abasa wa Tawalla and Idha al-Shamsu Kuwwirat will be beneath the wing (جناح) of Allah, among the gardens (الجنان), and in the shade of Allah and His generosity (كرامته), and in His Gardens (جنّاته). And that is not difficult for Allah, if Allah the Almighty wills.
